content.claim: Between-school ethnic segregation in Hungary strengthened substantially between 1980 and 2011, with high-poverty school attendance associated with lower reading scores, lower secondary completion rates, and lower likelihood of obtaining secondary school-leaving certificate.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Ethnic segregation finding is separately documented; specific causal link from 6/8-year gimnázium to Roma segregation is inferential and compositional, not directly measured
Recent quasi-experimental work measures poverty school effects, not gimnázium admission effects specifically
Segregation effects may reflect residential distribution and free choice policy rather than tracking mechanism alone
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
